publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for JUnit tests; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: JUnit tests: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? A green test with high line coverage proves the implementation is correct and makes boundary analysis unnecessary.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Derive JUnit cases and assertions from the public contract, isolate each fixture, and cover normal, boundary, and failure behavior deterministically.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Derive JUnit cases and assertions from the public contract, isolate each fixture, and cover normal, boundary, and failure behavior deterministically.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Derive JUnit cases and assertions from the public contract, isolate each fixture, and cover normal, boundary, and failure behavior deterministically.
A green test with high line coverage proves the implementation is correct and makes boundary analysis unnecessary.

answer.explanation: Derive JUnit cases and assertions from the public contract, isolate each fixture, and cover normal, boundary, and failure behavior deterministically.

items.title: JUnit tests: canonical checkpoint
items.prompt: Complete the source-aligned Java: Beginner to Intermediate correction with the exact canonical phrase used by this lesson.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint phrase from the operational correction, then read the complete sentence for semantic fit.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'public contract'. Derive JUnit cases and assertions from the public contract, isolate each fixture, and cover normal, boundary, and failure behavior deterministically.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the exact language rule. Derive JUnit cases and assertions from the public contract, isolate each fixture, and cover normal, boundary, and failure behavior deterministically.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical checkpoint phrase that completes the source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and whether additional spelling variants are technically equivalent.
